---
title: "analysis"
format: html
---
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)

dat <- readRDS("/Users/treniah/STAT3000/hw/hw4/data/dat.rds")
```

```
```
## Figure 1: National weekly burden

```{r}
#| eval: true

fig1_dat <- dat |>
  group_by(date) |>
  summarise(
    cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    hosp = sum(hosp, na.rm = TRUE),
    deaths = sum(deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = sum(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    cases_rate = cases / pop * 100000,
    hosp_rate = hosp / pop * 100000,
    deaths_rate = deaths / pop * 100000
  ) |>
  select(date, cases_rate, hosp_rate, deaths_rate) |>
  pivot_longer(-date, names_to = "metric", values_to = "rate")

ggplot(fig1_dat, aes(date, rate)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "US COVID-19 Weekly Burden",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Rate per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
## Figure 2: Case rates for selected states

```{r}
#| eval: true

top_states <- dat |>
  group_by(state) |>
  summarise(total_cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  slice_max(total_cases, n = 6) |>
  pull(state)

fig2_dat <- dat |>
  filter(state %in% top_states) |>
  group_by(date, state) |>
  summarise(
    cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = first(population),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(case_rate = cases / pop * 100000)

ggplot(fig2_dat, aes(date, case_rate, color = state)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Case Rates (Top States)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Cases per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
## Figure 3: Vaccination and booster progress

```{r}
#| eval: true

fig3_dat <- dat |>
  mutate(
    vax = as.numeric(vax),
    booster = as.numeric(booster),
    population = as.numeric(population)
  ) |>
  group_by(date) |>
  summarise(
    vax = sum(vax, na.rm = TRUE),
    booster = sum(booster, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = sum(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    vax_pct = vax / pop * 100,
    booster_pct = booster / pop * 100
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(vax_pct, booster_pct),
    names_to = "series",
    values_to = "percent"
  )

ggplot(fig3_dat, aes(date, percent, color = series)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "US Vaccination and Booster Progress",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Percent of Population"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Figure 4: Top 10 states by COVID-19 death rate

```{r}
#| eval: true

fig4_dat <- dat |>
  mutate(
    deaths = as.numeric(deaths),
    population = as.numeric(population)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(state), !is.na(deaths), !is.na(population)) |>
  group_by(state, state_name) |>
  summarise(
    deaths = sum(deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = max(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(death_rate = deaths / pop * 100000) |>
  filter(is.finite(death_rate)) |>
  arrange(desc(death_rate)) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

fig4_dat

ggplot(fig4_dat, aes(x = reorder(state_name, death_rate), y = death_rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "tomato") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 States by COVID-19 Death Rate (2020–2021)",
    x = "State",
    y = "Deaths per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Figure 5: Vaccination vs hospitalization during Delta wave

```{r}
#| eval: true

start_date <- as_date("2021-07-01")
end_date   <- as_date("2021-09-30")

# --- hospitalization during wave ---
fig5_hosp <- dat |>
  filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date) |>
  mutate(hosp = as.numeric(hosp)) |>
  group_by(state, state_name) |>
  summarise(
    hosp = sum(hosp, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = max(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(hosp_rate = hosp / pop * 100000)

# --- vaccination at end of period ---
fig5_vax <- dat |>
  filter(date == max(date)) |>
  mutate(vax = as.numeric(vax)) |>
  group_by(state, state_name) |>
  summarise(
    vax = max(vax, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = max(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(vax_rate = vax / pop * 100)

# --- combine ---
fig5_dat <- left_join(fig5_hosp, fig5_vax, by = c("state", "state_name")) |>
  filter(is.finite(hosp_rate), is.finite(vax_rate))

ggplot(fig5_dat, aes(x = vax_rate, y = hosp_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Vaccination vs Hospitalization (Delta Wave)",
    x = "Vaccination rate (%)",
    y = "Hospitalizations per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Figure 6: Booster coverage vs COVID-19 death rate

```{r}
#| eval: true

start_date <- as_date("2021-11-01")
end_date   <- as_date("2021-12-31")

# --- deaths during window ---
fig6_deaths <- dat |>
  filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date) |>
  mutate(deaths = as.numeric(deaths)) |>
  group_by(state, state_name) |>
  summarise(
    deaths = sum(deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = max(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(death_rate = deaths / pop * 100000)

# --- booster coverage at end of window ---
fig6_booster <- dat |>
  filter(date == max(date)) |>
  mutate(booster = as.numeric(booster)) |>
  group_by(state, state_name) |>
  summarise(
    booster = max(booster, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = max(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(booster_rate = booster / pop * 100)

# --- combine ---
fig6_dat <- left_join(fig6_booster, fig6_deaths, by = c("state", "state_name")) |>
  filter(is.finite(booster_rate), is.finite(death_rate))

ggplot(fig6_dat, aes(x = booster_rate, y = death_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Booster Coverage vs Death Rate (Late 2021)",
    x = "Booster coverage (%)",
    y = "Deaths per 100,000"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
